mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5 percent, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, driving negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in real economic activity, which compresses profit margins and triggers negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The joint decline of INDPRO and HOUST fails to predict negative 20d SPX returns in more than 50% of observed instances over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-rate environment, rising consumer price inflation erodes real disposable income and forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, which suppresses equity valuations over the next week.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS jointly fail to predict negative 5-day SPX returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating growth in real GDP directly expands corporate revenue bases, which translates into higher earnings per share and positive equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth rates show no statistically significant correlation with 60-day forward SPX returns (p > 0.10) across multiple decades.

mechanism_narrative: When total consumer credit outstanding falls below 700000, it indicates a credit crunch that restricts household purchasing power and drives negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: RSAFS levels below 700000 fail to predict negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of observed instances over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment combined with stagnant industrial production signals a structural demand shortfall that forces corporate margin compression, driving negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The joint behavior of UNRATE and INDPRO fails to predict negative 20d SPX returns in more than 50% of observed instances over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of tight monetary policy, a sharp rise in industrial production indicates supply-side resilience that supports corporate earnings and drives positive equity returns over the next week.
regime_hint: restrictive monetary policy with supply resilience
failure_condition: INDPRO rises fail to predict positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.0 percent.
